content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement • • Statements of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5662          322-5660              322-5901              322-6441              322-6444


February 24, 1981


Jonathan Smith
San Francisco Bay Conservation and
Development Commission
30 Van Ness Avenue
San Francisco, CA 94102

Dear Mr. Smith:

     I apoligize for the delay in sending you the material you
requested.  The copies enclosed represent all the information
I was able to find on the subject of consultants.  I hope this
will be of some help.

     Should you have more specific questions, please feel free
to contact me.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]
                                        Barbara A. Milman
                                        Staff Counsel
                                        Legal Division

State of California

Memorandum

To : California Fair Political Practices Commission
Attn: Mr. Tom Sobel
P. O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804

Date : January 22, 1981

File No.:

From : San Francisco Bay Conservation and Development Commission
30 Van Ness Avenue
San Francisco, California 94102

We are presently preparing to enter into a contract with the Suisun
Resource Conservation District to provide services called for in Section
9962 and Section 29101(d) of the Public Resources Code. These services
include the analysis of several variables in determining the size of inlet
and outlet structures for 82 duck clubs in the primary management area of
the Suisun Marsh. They also include the preparation of a report regarding
the determination, an operations manual, an analysis of each club's filling
and draining capability, and recommended sizes of the filling and draining
facilities for each club.

The question has arisen of whether or not the Conservation District or
any of its employees or subcontractors would have to file a statement of
economic interests under the Fair Political Practices Act. After reviewing
the Commission's definition of a "consultant" contained at 2 Cal. Admin. Code
Section 18700(a)(2) and discussing the application of that definition with
Mr. Tom Sobel, we have decided that the Conservation District and its employees
and subcontractors do not qualify as consultants and are therefore not required
under our conflict of interest code to file a statement of economic interests.
If you have any concerns regarding this conclusion, please contact us immediately.
If we do not hear from you, we shall not change our decision.

[signature]
JONATHAN T. SMITH

The copies enclosed represent all the information I was able to find on the subject of consultants. I hope this will be of some help. Should you have more specific questions, please feel free to contact me. Sincerely, Barbara A. Milman Staff Counsel Legal Division This memorandum is from the San Francisco Bay Conservation and Development Commission to the California Fair Political Practices Commission regarding a contract with the Suisun Resource Conservation District. The commission explains that they are preparing to enter into a contract for services related to duck clubs in the Suisun Marsh, including analysis of inlet and outlet structures, preparation of a report, an operations manual, and analysis of filling and draining capabilities for each club. The memo then addresses whether the Conservation District or its employees or subcontractors would need to file statements of economic interests under the Fair Political Practices Act. After reviewing the Commission's definition of a "consultant" contained at 2 Cal. Admin. Code Section 18700(a)(2) and discussing the application of this definition with Mr. Tom Sobel, the commission concludes that the Conservation District and its employees and subcontractors do not qualify as consultants and are therefore not required to file a statement of economic interests. The memo states that if there are any concerns regarding this conclusion, the commission should be contacted immediately. If no response is received, the decision will remain unchanged. The memo is signed by Jonathan T. Smith.
